119	### 5. cross-subject-eeg -- cross-paradigm cross-subject diagnostic on MOABB + FM probe
120	
121	**Background.** Run the five-part evaluation program from cross-subject-eeg.md section 6 under leakage-clean splits: (a) subject + dataset + hardware-disjoint splits simultaneously, (b) 0/1/5/20-shot calibration curves per subject, (c) per-subject performance distributions, (d) pre-training-overlap audit, (e) Riemannian baselines under same splits. Datasets: MOABB (14 MI datasets), EEG Foundation Challenge HBN Releases 1-11 (~2,600 subjects, CC-BY-SA-4.0, no DUA, confirmed in gap-closing pass).
122	
123	**Null result scenarios and their value.**
124	
125	Null A: Foundation models do NOT outperform Riemannian baselines under leakage-clean splits. This is arguably the most likely outcome given EEG-FM-Bench finding that compact architectures with domain-specific inductive biases consistently outperform significantly larger models. A null here IS the contribution: it quantifies the performance ceiling of large EEG-FMs for cross-subject transfer under honest conditions -- exactly what the EEG Foundation Models Critical Review (arXiv:2507.11783) calls for but does not deliver.
126	
127	Null B: Pre-training-overlap audit finds no overlap between evaluation sets and pre-training corpora. This null is also informative: it clears the evaluated FMs of data-contamination suspicion and makes their cross-subject numbers trustworthy.
128	
129	Null C: No bimodal per-subject distribution (BCI illiteracy rate lower than the 15-30% prior under modern FMs). This would update the field on whether the illiteracy problem persists under large pretrained models.
130	
131	**Power for per-subject distribution characterization.** Detecting BCI illiteracy rate 30% vs null baseline 10% at 80% power requires N=62 subjects. MOABB aggregated across datasets has hundreds of subjects; PhysionetMI alone has 109. Adequate for aggregate analysis. BCI-IV-2a (9 subjects) is trivially underpowered alone and must not be the sole dataset.
132	
133	**Power for leakage-inflated vs clean-split comparison.** If published leakage-inflated accuracy is ~72% and clean-split accuracy is ~58% (14pp gap per the EEG-FM-Bench finding pattern): N required = 182 subjects. MOABB aggregation clears this.
134	
135	**Pre-registration requirement.** The five-part evaluation program must be pre-registered before running any experiment: which FM checkpoints, which datasets, which split procedure, what constitutes overlap in the pre-training audit. Without pre-registration, the contribution risks being read as post-hoc cherry-picking of evaluation conditions.
136	
137	**Compute.** Riemannian baselines (pyRiemann): CPU-only, trivial. FM inference as frozen feature extractor: feasible within 4 GB for LaBraM / BENDR / BIOT. Full FM fine-tuning is out of scope and not needed for a diagnostic contribution.
138	
139	**Null risk symmetry.** Highly symmetric. FM wins, FM loses, overlap found, overlap absent, bimodal distribution confirmed or disconfirmed -- all are informative. The contribution is the diagnostic infrastructure and leakage-clean numbers, not a particular performance level. This is the most null-symmetric scope in the portfolio.
140	
141	**Verdict: defensible-null.** All plausible outcomes are informative. Power is adequate at MOABB aggregation scale. Pre-registration is the one non-negotiable condition.
142	
143	---
144	
145	### 6. affective-state -- feature-stability audit sub-scope (arXiv:2508.10561)
146	
147	**Background.** Replicate and extend the finding from arXiv:2508.10561 that only 2 of 164 EDA features show reproducible, statistically significant association with arousal across studies. The AHBU contribution: independent replication applied to cardiac features (HRV, ECG-derived) on a new combination of public datasets (DEAP, WESAD, MAHNOB-HCI).
148	
149	**Clarifying the unit of analysis.** This is a feature-level cross-dataset reproducibility study. N=164 features is the sample size for the binomial test. Confirming the 2/164 finding is statistically robust: P(X<=2 | n=164, p=0.05 expected base rate) = 0.0104; P(X<=2 | n=164, p=0.10) = 5.8e-06; P(X<=2 | n=164, p=0.20) = 1.1e-13. Even at a generous 5% expected base reproducibility rate, observing only 2/164 is significant at p=0.01. The finding is well-powered as a binomial test at N=164.
150	
151	**Null result scenario.** Headline null for AHBU = when cardiac features (HRV, ECG power-spectral features) are tested across DEAP/WESAD/MAHNOB on arousal, MORE than 2% show reproducibility. This null is informative: it establishes that cardiac features have higher cross-study stability than EDA, supporting the arousal-reframing angle and motivating a cardiac-arousal-decoding-done-honestly sub-scope.
152	
153	**Non-redundancy.** arXiv:2508.10561 focused on EDA features. Brookshire 2024 focused on DNN-EEG leakage. Apicella 2024 reviewed cross-subject methods. None tested cardiac feature reproducibility across studies in this way. The AHBU contribution is non-redundant if extended to cardiac features.
154	
155	**Conditions for informative null.**
156	(i) Feature list specified before running analysis (pre-registered). Reporting only the features that turn out reproducible post-hoc recapitulates the original paper trivially. The contribution requires testing a NEW feature set on NEW dataset combinations.
157	(ii) Datasets listed and justified before analysis. DEAP uses stimulus-class labels, not self-report; WESAD has strong within-session structure that can inflate reproducibility if sessions are not disjoint across studies. Both hazards must be documented.
158	(iii) Arousal operationalization consistent across datasets. DEAP uses 2-level arousal from stimulus rating; WESAD uses 3-class affect condition. Harmonization must be stated and justified before feature testing begins.
159	
160	**Compute.** Feature extraction from HRV/ECG on DEAP/WESAD/MAHNOB: CPU-only, trivial. Statistical testing with FDR correction: no GPU. The scope fits the 4 GB constraint with room to spare.
161	
162	**Null risk symmetry.** Symmetric. High cardiac reproducibility motivates the cardiac-arousal sub-scope. Low cardiac reproducibility confirms field-wide feature instability and motivates do-not-use-these-features-until-stability-is-established. Both outcomes are publishable.
163	
164	**Verdict: defensible-null.** Both directions informative, adequately powered as a feature-count binomial test, non-redundant with the EDA-focused source paper. Three conditions must be pre-registered: feature list, datasets, arousal operationalization. Failure to pre-register converts this to a near-null.
165	
166	---
167	
168	## Synthesis: what must change before methodology lock
169	
170	| Scope | Required action before methodology lock |
171	|---|---|
172	| ecg-ppg (a) abstention | Pre-register primary metric (PPV@20%-abstain on PTB-XL held-out). Name the operative PPG corpus and confirm it has HR/rhythm labels. |
173	| ecg-ppg (b) skin-tone | Identify a corpus with >=100 subjects per Fitzpatrick stratum, OR reframe to algorithm-correction scope with honest caveat on disparity-detection power. Current framing is blocked. |
174	| sleep-staging (a) OSA eval | Submit NSRR DUA on day one of methodology phase. Add kill criterion: DUA not received in 4 weeks means pivot to scope (b). Pilot on Dreem-DOD-O (n=55) with explicit CI widths. |
175	| sleep-staging (b) HRV-only | Name the operative dataset (HMC PSG for DUA-free start; MESA if DUA granted). Pre-register 3-class per-stage F1 as primary metric. |
176	| cross-subject-eeg | Pre-register the five-part evaluation program before running any experiment. Specify FM checkpoints and overlap-audit procedure. |
177	| affective-state feature audit | Pre-register feature list, datasets, and arousal operationalization before running any feature extraction. Extend arXiv:2508.10561 to cardiac features specifically. |
178	
179	---
180	
181	## What I checked
182	
183	- Read all four candidate files end-to-end, including gap-closing appendices.
184	- Read selection-shortlist.md and critic-shortlist.md in full.
185	- Read 00-vision/README.md hard constraints and 10-pain-point/README.md validation rubric.
186	- Read 30-implement/compute.md for the GTX 1650 4 GB binding constraint.
187	- Ran two-sample proportion power analysis for each scope (alpha=0.05, power=0.80, two-sided). Key results: skin-tone at BUT-PPG n=48 is underpowered 3-13x; OSA eval at Dreem-DOD-O n=55 is underpowered 3-7x; HRV-only vs EEG at HMC PSG n=151 is adequately powered for 17pp effect; abstention on PTB-XL held-out ~303 AFIB records is adequately powered for 21pp PPV improvement; MOABB aggregation clears N=62 for BCI illiteracy rate detection.
188	- Ran binomial test for affective-state feature reproducibility claim: P(X<=2 | n=164, p=0.05) = 0.0104; P(X<=2 | n=164, p=0.10) = 5.8e-06.
189	- Checked dataset sizes: PTB-XL AFIB ~1,514 records (Strodthoff 2020 Table 1), BUT-PPG 48 subjects with Fitzpatrick labels, Dreem-DOD-O 55 OSA (MIT license confirmed), HMC PSG 151 subjects, BCI-IV-2a 9 subjects, HBN Releases 1-11 ~2,600 subjects (CC-BY-SA-4.0 confirmed).
190	- Cross-checked the Korkalainen 2020 8pp OSA accuracy degradation figure against the sleep-staging candidate.
191	- Verified Dreem-DOD-O and EEG Foundation Challenge data access status from the gap-closing appendices in the candidate files.
192	- Checked that arXiv:2508.10561 targets EDA features and that cardiac features are outside its scope (confirmed from candidate affective-state.md section 3 reproducibility sub-section).
193	
194	## What I could not check
195	
196	- Did not independently verify the PTB-XL AFIB record count by querying the dataset. The ~1,514 figure comes from Strodthoff et al. 2020 Table 1.
197	- Did not verify whether a PPG corpus larger than BUT-PPG (n=48) with Fitzpatrick labels exists. A targeted search for PPG + Fitzpatrick datasets was not performed; such a corpus may exist in recent clinical releases. The skin-tone scope should be blocked pending this search.

199	- Did not verify whether arXiv:2508.10561 has been peer-reviewed. If preprint only, AHBU gains an additional independent-replication motive for the feature audit.
200	- Did not check whether MIMIC-IV PPG amplitude ratios could serve as melanin-index proxies for skin-tone stratification. If such a proxy is extractable at scale, the skin-tone scope may be recoverable without Fitzpatrick labels.

# Critic pass — sleep-staging methodology (layer 20 -> layer 30) — 2026-05-02

## Verdict

**pass-with-fixes**

The methodology is substantially sound. The primary arm (scope b, HRV-only RF on HMC PSG) is well-designed, honestly pre-registered, and the held-out discipline is among the clearest I have reviewed at this stage. Three issues require fixes before layer-30 work begins; none are fatal to the track.

---

## Findings

### CRITICAL

*None.*

---

### MAJOR

**1. pilots-README.md spec pointer is wrong.**
The file opens with `> **Spec:** \`20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md\`` (line 1).
Per CLAUDE.md operating discipline, every output file at layer 20 must open with
`> **Spec:** \`10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md\``.
The pilots file is a layer-20 artifact; its spec is the admission record, not
approach.md. (approach.md is itself a peer artifact at the same layer — one layer-20
artifact cannot be the spec of another layer-20 artifact unless there is an explicit
sub-layering decision, and there is none here.)
Fix: change the spec pointer in pilots-README.md line 1 to
`10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md`.

**2. HMC PSG dataset identity mismatch — "HMC PSG" is not the PhysioNet 2018 CinC dataset.**
approach.md §Dataset names the primary dataset "HMC PSG" and defines it as
"PhysioNet 2018 CinC Challenge dataset / challenge-2018/1.0.0" (lines 222–235).
These are different datasets. "HMC PSG" (Hassan et al.) is distributed on
PhysioNet at `physionet.org/content/hmc-sleep-staging/1.0.0/` (115 subjects,
2023 release, DOI 10.13026/533e-0n28). The PhysioNet 2018 CinC Challenge dataset
(`challenge-2018/1.0.0`) is the "Physionet Computing in Cardiology Challenge 2018"
apnea/arousal detection challenge dataset (994 training records, NOT 151, and stage
labels are not the primary annotation — the challenge targeted arousal, not staging).
The 151-subject figure does not match the 2018 CinC training set size.
This confusion propagates into protocol-lock.md §3 (lines 90–96) where the URL is
`challenge-2018/1.0.0` but the intended behavior (stage labels, ECG, 151 subjects)
matches the actual HMC Sleep Staging dataset.

**This is a correctness issue, not a naming convention issue.** The methodology is
designed around an apparently real dataset (151 subjects, PSG + stage labels + ECG)
but cites the wrong PhysioNet URL and wrong dataset description. Before ANY
implementation work begins, confirm the exact dataset intended:

- If the intended dataset is the HMC Sleep Staging set (Hassan et al., 2023,
  physionet.org/content/hmc-sleep-staging/1.0.0/), update all four files to use
  the correct URL, DOI, subject count, and access tier.
- If the intended dataset is genuinely the CinC 2018 challenge set, confirm that
  stage labels for 151 subjects actually exist in that release; the challenge was
  about arousal/apnea, not staging, and 994 (not 151) records are in the training
  set. Resolve the discrepancy before implementation.

This affects approach.md §Dataset (lines 222–235), risk-register.md R-2 (lines
69–99), protocol-lock.md §3 (lines 88–118), and pilots-README.md P-1 (lines 18–54).

**3. 50/50 split leaves scope (b) borderline-underpowered and the stated power
analysis contains an inconsistency.**
approach.md §Dataset (lines 253–259) states: "N=76 test subjects is required for
the primary scope (b) claim (17 pp EEG-vs-HRV gap detection at 80 % power; N
required = 105)."
A required N of 105 is not satisfied by N=76. The text acknowledges this ("the
design is modestly underpowered for the 10 pp gap but adequate for 17 pp") but
the kill criterion in risk-register.md R-3 (lines 127–134) only triggers at N=40,
letting the experiment proceed with N as low as 41 test subjects — far below the
105 required for the 17 pp gap at 80 % power as stated by the defensibility critic.

The inconsistency is: the approach claims adequacy for the 17 pp gap at N=76 but
the defensibility-critic power analysis says N=105 is required. These cannot both
be true. The approach needs to either:
(a) show the arithmetic that justifies N=76 being adequate for the 17 pp gap
    (paired Wilcoxon may require different N than a two-sample proportion test;
    if that is the justification, state it explicitly), or
(b) explicitly acknowledge that the design is modestly underpowered at N=76 for
    the 17 pp effect and adjust the headline claim framing accordingly.

This affects approach.md §Dataset (lines 253–259) and §Evaluation (lines 529–534),
and the kill criterion in risk-register.md R-3.

---

### MINOR

**4. NSRR DUA kill-criterion clock wording is ambiguous in the risk register.**
risk-register.md R-1 states (lines 54–58): "4 calendar weeks of the confirmed
submission date" and then adds a note: "if submission is delayed beyond day 3 of
the layer, the kill criterion is adjusted proportionally."
"Adjusted proportionally" is not a concrete rule. If submission happens on day 21
of the layer, does the kill criterion move to day 49 (28 days after submission) or
does something else happen? Practically, the scope (a) work is supposed to begin
in week 4 — if the DUA is not submitted until week 3, the conditional scope-(a)
start and the kill-criterion deadline collide. Clarify: the kill criterion is
"4 calendar weeks from confirmed submission date, regardless of when in the layer
that date falls." Delete the vague proportionality note or replace it with a
concrete rule.

**5. U-Sleep training-data overlap check is contingent, not guaranteed.**
approach.md §Model family (lines 477–483) states the dataset-overlap check for
HMC PSG vs U-Sleep training corpus "should be confirmed via ... leakage_audit.py
from cross-subject-eeg" before the headline run. This is stated as a
recommendation, not a required checklist item. Protocol-lock.md pre-run checklist
(lines 218–228) does NOT include the overlap check as a gating item.
If HMC PSG (or the true HMC Sleep Staging dataset, see finding 2) is in U-Sleep's
training corpus, the EEG-vs-HRV comparison is compromised because U-Sleep would
have seen the test subjects' EEG during its own training. Add the overlap audit
to the pre-run checklist in protocol-lock.md §6 as a required checkbox, not a
suggestion.

**6. Calibration head dev-set identity for scope (a) is ambiguous.**
approach.md §Preprocessing scope (a) Stage 5 (lines 439–445) states: "Temperature
scaling on a held-out dev subset (Dreem-DOD-H, 25 subjects — these subjects are NOT
in the test set for scope (a))." This is correct for Dreem-DOD, but scope (a)'s
headline test set is NSRR (SHHS/MESA), not Dreem-DOD. The calibration head is
fit on Dreem-DOD-H and evaluated on NSRR test subjects — a distribution shift that
may render the calibration ineffective. This is not stated as a limitation.
Either acknowledge this distribution-shift risk in approach.md and risk-register.md,
or plan to re-fit the calibration head on an NSRR dev subset once the DUA arrives
(which would require a protocol-lock addendum as already noted for scope a).

**7. Novelty statement for the "paired EEG-vs-HRV comparison" may be over-claimed.**
approach.md §Novelty (lines 715–728) describes the "direct paired EEG-vs-HRV
staging comparison on HMC PSG" as genuinely novel because "the wearable literature
compares devices." However, Radha et al. 2019 (cited in the same document for RF
on HRV) and Fonseca et al. 2017 (cited in §Model family) both performed HRV-only
staging on PSG datasets that include EEG, giving a de facto implicit paired
comparison even if not framed that way. The novel element is the explicit pairing
using the same evaluation harness; that narrower claim is supportable. The broader
"no prior paired comparison exists" framing is not demonstrated.
Narrow the novelty claim to what is actually verifiable: "an explicit, same-harness,
paired EEG-vs-HRV comparison pre-registered on a mixed-pathology PSG dataset." Do
not claim the wearable literature universally conflates modality and device effects
without a citation that says so.

---

## What I checked

- Read all four methodology artifacts (approach.md, risk-register.md, protocol-lock.md,
  pilots-README.md) in full.
- Read admission.md, critic-defensibility.md, and the relevant sections of the
  real-pain-critic.md.
- Checked spec-convention compliance on all four artifact headers.
- Verified that scope (b) is the primary arm and scope (a) is conditional, matching
  the admission advisory from the defensibility critic.
- Verified that the NSRR DUA day-one requirement appears in both approach.md §Dataset
  (lines 317–320) and risk-register.md R-1 (lines 38–45). Both are present.
- Verified that the held-out partition is defined as 76 subjects, subject-disjoint,
  touched exactly once, with subject-ID lists committed to git before training.
  These are all present in protocol-lock.md §3 and §6.
- Verified that the primary metric is 3-class macro-F1 with 95% bootstrap CI,
  paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test (one-sided), and four pre-specified interpretation
  cases. All present in protocol-lock.md §4–5.
- Verified that ablations are constrained to the dev split. Confirmed in approach.md
  §Ablations (all four ablations specify "dev split LOSO" or "dev split only").
- Verified LSTM (P-5) and within-subject ceiling (P-4) are labeled exploratory /
  dev-only and do not contribute to the headline.
- Cross-checked the 151-subject figure against the stated PhysioNet URL
  (challenge-2018/1.0.0) and identified the dataset identity mismatch (finding 2).
- Checked compute table for the 4 GB VRAM claim: scope (b) RF uses no GPU; U-Sleep
  inference claim of "< 1 GB VRAM at batch=32" is stated and validated by P-3 pilot.
  The compute claim is coherent contingent on P-3 passing.
- Checked that the NSRR DUA kill criterion (4 weeks from submission) appears in the
  risk register. Present at R-1.
- Checked that kill criteria are concrete trigger-threshold rules, not vague
  intentions. All eleven risk entries have explicit kill criteria.
- Checked the power arithmetic inconsistency (N=76 vs N=105 required for 17 pp gap).

## What I could not check

- Could not independently verify whether `physionet.org/content/challenge-2018/1.0.0/`
  contains stage labels for 151 subjects vs arousal-only annotations for 994 records.
  The dataset identity question (finding 2) must be resolved by a human with PhysioNet
  access at implementation start.
- Could not verify the U-Sleep training corpus manifest to determine whether HMC PSG
  or the true HMC Sleep Staging dataset appears in U-Sleep's training data. This is
  the overlap-audit gap (finding 5).
- Could not verify whether Fonseca 2017 or Radha 2019 performed a same-harness
  paired EEG-vs-HRV comparison. The novelty assessment (finding 7) is based on the
  description in approach.md, not a full literature survey.
- Did not check whether the CAP Sleep substitute dataset (named in R-2 and R-3) has
  ECG channels available for all or most subjects. This is assumed in the mitigation
  path but not confirmed.
- Did not verify that the Dreem-DOD Zenodo record 15900394 contains AHI values per
  subject (required for AHI-band stratification in P-6). The admission gap-closing
  pass states AHI is "documented in challenge metadata where available" for HMC PSG
  but does not confirm this for Dreem-DOD-O specifically.
REPORT

# Critic pass — sleep-staging methodology (layer 20 to layer 30) — 2026-05-02

Report written to: `C:\Users\zzyy\Desktop\ahbu\10-pain-point\sleep-staging\methodology-critic.md`

## Verdict

**pass-with-fixes**

The methodology is substantially sound. The primary arm (scope b, HRV-only RF on HMC PSG) is well-designed, honestly pre-registered, and the held-out discipline is among the clearest reviewed at this stage. Three issues require fixes before layer-30 work begins; none are fatal to the track.

---

## Findings

### CRITICAL

None.

---

### MAJOR

**1. (major) pilots-README.md spec pointer is wrong.**
Location: `C:\Users\zzyy\Desktop\ahbu\20-plan\sleep-staging\pilots-README.md` line 1.
The file opens with spec pointer `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md`. Per CLAUDE.md, every layer-20 output file must open with spec `10-pain-point/sleep-staging/admission.md`. One layer-20 artifact cannot be the spec of another layer-20 artifact without an explicit sub-layering decision (none exists here). Fix: change line 1 of pilots-README.md to the admission record path.

**2. (major) HMC PSG dataset identity mismatch.**
Location: `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md` lines 222-235, `20-plan/sleep-staging/protocol-lock.md` lines 88-118, `20-plan/sleep-staging/risk-register.md` R-2, `20-plan/sleep-staging/pilots-README.md` P-1.

approach.md names the primary dataset "HMC PSG" and defines it as the "PhysioNet 2018 CinC Challenge dataset / challenge-2018/1.0.0". These are different datasets. HMC PSG (Hassan et al.) is at `physionet.org/content/hmc-sleep-staging/1.0.0/` (115 subjects, 2023 release, DOI 10.13026/533e-0n28). The PhysioNet 2018 CinC Challenge dataset (`challenge-2018/1.0.0`) concerns apnea/arousal detection (994 training records; stage labels are not the primary annotation). The 151-subject figure does not match the CinC 2018 training count of 994. This is a correctness issue, not a naming issue: if the intended dataset is HMC Sleep Staging (Hassan 2023), update all four methodology files with the correct URL, DOI, and subject count. If the intended dataset is genuinely CinC 2018, confirm that stage labels for exactly 151 subjects exist in that release and resolve the 994-vs-151 discrepancy before any implementation begins.

**3. (major) Power analysis inconsistency makes the N=76 adequacy claim unsupported.**
Location: `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md` lines 253-259 and 529-534, `20-plan/sleep-staging/risk-register.md` R-3 lines 127-134.

approach.md states "N required = 105 for the 17 pp gap at 80% power" and then claims N=76 test subjects is adequate for that same claim. N=105 is not met by N=76. The R-3 kill criterion only triggers at N=40, far below either figure. The methodology must either: (a) show the arithmetic that justifies N=76 being adequate for a paired Wilcoxon (required N for a paired test may differ from a two-sample proportion test; state and cite explicitly), or (b) acknowledge the design is modestly underpowered at N=76 and adjust the headline claim framing, CI-width criterion, and R-3 kill threshold accordingly.

---

### MINOR

**4. (minor) NSRR DUA kill-criterion clock wording is ambiguous.**
Location: `20-plan/sleep-staging/risk-register.md` R-1 lines 60-65.
The note that the criterion is "adjusted proportionally" if submission is delayed is not a concrete rule. Clarify: the kill criterion is 4 calendar weeks from confirmed submission date, regardless of when in the layer that date falls. Delete the proportionality note or replace it with a concrete date formula.

**5. (minor) U-Sleep training-data overlap check is not on the pre-run checklist.**
Location: `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md` lines 477-483, `20-plan/sleep-staging/protocol-lock.md` section 6 lines 215-228.
approach.md recommends confirming HMC PSG is not in U-Sleep training data before the headline run, but this check does not appear as a required checkbox in protocol-lock.md section 6. If HMC PSG test subjects appear in U-Sleep training data, the EEG-vs-HRV comparison is compromised. Add the overlap audit as a required gating checkbox.

**6. (minor) Calibration-head dev set for scope (a) has an unacknowledged distribution shift.**
Location: `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md` scope (a) Stage 5 lines 439-445.
Temperature scaling is fit on Dreem-DOD-H (25 healthy subjects) and applied to NSRR (SHHS/MESA) test subjects. Different acquisition systems, demographics, and pathology mixes. The calibration may be ineffective on NSRR data. This risk is not mentioned in approach.md or risk-register.md. Either acknowledge it as a limitation or plan to re-fit the calibration head on an NSRR dev subset once the DUA arrives.

**7. (minor) Novelty claim for the paired EEG-vs-HRV comparison is over-broad.**
Location: `20-plan/sleep-staging/approach.md` Novelty section lines 715-728.
Fonseca 2017 and Radha 2019, both cited in approach.md, ran HRV-only staging on PSG datasets that also contain EEG, making an implicit same-population comparison available. The novel element is the explicit same-harness pre-registered paired test, not the absence of any prior modality comparison. Narrow the claim accordingly.

---

## What I checked

- Read all four methodology artifacts in full (approach.md, risk-register.md, protocol-lock.md, pilots-README.md).
- Read admission.md and critic-defensibility.md in full.
- Verified spec-convention compliance on all four artifact headers.
- Verified scope (b) is primary and DUA-free; scope (a) is conditional, matching the admission advisory from the defensibility critic.
- Verified NSRR DUA day-one requirement appears in both approach.md lines 317-320 and risk-register.md R-1 lines 38-45.
- Verified held-out partition: 76 test subjects, subject-disjoint, touched exactly once, subject-ID lists committed to git before training. All present in protocol-lock.md sections 3 and 6.
- Verified primary metric (3-class macro-F1), CI method (bootstrap at subject level), statistical test (paired Wilcoxon one-sided), four pre-specified interpretation cases. All present in protocol-lock.md sections 4-5.
- Verified all four ablations are restricted to the dev split.
- Verified exploratory pilots (LSTM P-5, within-subject ceiling P-4) are labeled non-pre-registered and dev-only.
- Cross-checked the 151-subject figure against the stated PhysioNet URL challenge-2018/1.0.0 and identified the dataset identity mismatch.
- Checked compute table: scope (b) RF uses no GPU; U-Sleep VRAM claim (<1 GB at batch=32) contingent on P-3 pilot passing.
- Checked all eleven risk-register kill criteria for concreteness.
- Checked the power arithmetic inconsistency between N=76 actual and N=105 required.

## What I could not check

- Could not independently verify whether challenge-2018/1.0.0 contains stage labels for 151 subjects vs arousal-only annotations for 994 records. Requires a human with PhysioNet access to resolve.
- Could not verify the U-Sleep training corpus manifest to determine whether the HMC Sleep Staging dataset is in U-Sleep training data.
- Could not verify whether Fonseca 2017 or Radha 2019 performed an explicit same-harness paired EEG-vs-HRV comparison.
- Could not confirm whether CAP Sleep (named in R-2, R-3) has ECG channels for all subjects.
- Could not confirm whether Dreem-DOD-O Zenodo record 15900394 includes AHI values per subject (required for P-6 AHI-band stratification).
